name: San Francisco Bay Money Services
description_short: San Francisco Bay Money Services offers international money transfers, phone reloads, and document services. Currently temporarily suspended check-cashing and money order services.
description_long:
San Francisco Bay Money Services is located at 4830 Mission Street, Suite 101 in San Francisco, California. The company positions itself as a multi-service hub combining money transfer, computer repair, and document services under one roof with affiliated businesses (San Francisco Bay Computer Services and San Francisco General Services).

The company's primary active services include international money transfers through Ria Money Transfer, Boss Revolution, Viamericas, and Western Union. They also offer phone top-ups and reloads for cell phones. Secondary services include photocopies (black/white and color), scanning, faxing, lamination, internet cafe access ($5/hour), LiveScan fingerprinting by appointment, computer repair (Apple and PC), cell phone screen/battery repair, website design, data recovery, and business consulting.

The company emphasizes transparency and control in transactions, with messaging stating "no hidden fees" and commitment to clear communication. They position themselves as a serious, customer-focused business that cares about efficiency and honesty. The website indicates they serve customers needing services to Central America and the Philippines, as well as domestic U.S. transfers.

A significant caveat is that the company has temporarily suspended two of their historically core services: check-cashing and money orders. The website does not provide a timeline for when these services will resume. Current operations focus primarily on money transfer and ancillary services. The website contains some grammatical inconsistencies and formatting issues that may affect user confidence.
pros: Offers multiple international money transfer providers (Western Union, Ria, Boss Revolution, Viamericas) providing customer choice
Co-located with computer repair services in same building for convenient one-stop access
Explicitly advertises "no hidden fees" and claims transparency in transactions
Provides phone reloads and top-ups for maintaining cell phone service
Offers LiveScan fingerprinting services by appointment
Provides data recovery for non-physically damaged drives
Website design services available starting at $650 for personal use
cons: Check-cashing services temporarily unavailable with no stated timeline for resumption
Money order services temporarily unavailable with no stated timeline for resumption
Website contains grammatical errors and formatting issues that may reduce user confidence
Limited online presence and no apparent online money transfer capability despite offering the service
best_for: Immigrants sending money to Central America or the Philippines
Customers needing international money transfers with multiple provider options
Individuals seeking phone reloads and top-ups in the San Francisco Mission District
Small business owners needing website design services on a budget
diagnosis: Best for customers in the San Francisco Mission District needing international money transfers to Central America or the Philippines, or those seeking phone reloads with access to ancillary services. Primary caveat: core check-cashing and money order services are temporarily unavailable, which may limit utility for customers traditionally using such services.
services: International money transfers (Western Union, Ria Money Transfer, Boss Revolution, Viameriques)
Cell phone screen and battery replacement
Website design ($650+ for personal, $850+ for business)
